title: Bhaktavatsala Perumal Temple, Thirunindravur

overview.short: Sri Bhaktavatsala Perumal temple is a Hindu temple, located at Thirunindravur, a western suburb of Chennai, India.
overview.description: Sri Bhaktavatsala Perumal temple is a Hindu temple, located at Thirunindravur, a western suburb of Chennai, India. It is dedicated to the Hindu deity Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Bhaktavatsala Perumal and his consort Lakshmi as Ennai Petra Thayar. topPlaces.name: Hridayaleeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Sri Hirudhayaleeswarar sivan temple is a Hindu temple at Thirunindravur, Chennai, India.

topPlaces.name: Tiruninravur Lake
topPlaces.description: Thiruninravur Lake or Thiruninravur Aeri, is a lake spread over 330 hectares in Thiruninravur, Chennai, India. It is one of the largest lakes in the western part of the city.

topPlaces.name: Thiruninravur
topPlaces.description: Thirunindravur (also spelt Tiruninravur) is a suburban town and locality in the western part of Chennai city and falls under Tiruvallur district, Tamil Nadu. It lies within the Chennai Metropolitan Area (Greater Chennai), around 30 kilometers from the city center at Parry's Corner.

topPlaces.name: Jaya Group of Colleges
topPlaces.description: The Jaya Group of Colleges (Tamil: ஜெயா கல்லூரிகள்) consists of more than 20 education institutions including two higher secondary schools and seven professional course institutions in the suburbs of Thiruninravur, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India. The colleges are run by a trust named Jaya Education Trust.

topPlaces.name: Veppampattu
topPlaces.description: Veppampattu is a town and one of the fastest developing suburban areas in Chennai City. It is Western part of the suburbs of the Metropolitan city Chennai in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu between Chennai and Arakkonam on the Chennai - Arakkonam railway line.

topPlaces.name: Pakkam
topPlaces.description: Pakkam is a village which is located in Chennai in the Thiruvallur District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is situated around 34 km from Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Ayathur
topPlaces.description: Ayathur is a village in Tiruvallur Taluk, Tiruvallur District, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located to the west of Palavedu Lake, about 30 kilometres west of Chennai, and 10 kilometres east of the district capital Tiruvallur.

topPlaces.name: Pattabiram
topPlaces.description: Pattabiram is a neighbourhood situated on the western part of Chennai, India. The suburb, which falls under Avadi Municipal Corporation, is about 25 km from the Chennai Central railway station.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bhaktavatsala Perumal Temple, Thirunindravur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bhaktavatsala Perumal Temple, Thirunindravur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bhaktavatsala Perumal Temple, Thirunindravur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (20 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (27 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bhaktavatsala Perumal Temple, Thirunindravur?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bhaktavatsala Perumal Temple, Thirunindravur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bhaktavatsala Perumal Temple, Thirunindravur?
faq.a: Stays in Bhaktavatsala Perumal Temple, Thirunindravur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bhaktavatsala Perumal Temple, Thirunindravur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hridayaleeswarar Temple, Tiruninravur Lake, Thiruninravur, Jaya Group of Colleges, Veppampattu.
